key_sections: 17.0 Waiver of Jury Trial and Punitive Damages

provisions.burden: Non-exclusive license at specified location only
Franchisor reserves all rights to operate, franchise any business including competitors, anywhere, anytime
Restricted Area Provision optional, time-limited, extensive carve-outs
New construction: 23 years. Conversion: 10-20 years. Non-renewable. Re-licensing at sole discretion with materially different terms possible.
Monthly royalty 5% GRR + program fee 4% GRR (may increase +1% over term)
Must transmit payments by wire/electronic transfer at franchisee's cost
Must pay all other fees as specified in FDD Items 5 and 6
Must reimburse franchisor for sales/use/gross receipts taxes on franchise payments
Franchisor may set off any amount owed against any payment or credit to franchisee (including affiliate obligations)
Must obtain franchisor consent for architect, designer, GC, major subs
Plans must be approved; no changes without advance consent; no construction until approved
New dev: start within 16 months, complete within 36 months of approval. Extensions $10K each.
May not open without written authorization. Unauthorized opening = $5,000/day liquidated damages.
Must comply with FRCM renovation schedule and any required refurbishment at franchisee's cost
Must complete required training programs and pay fees plus travel/lodging

29 operational requirements including: Standards compliance, approved management, QA inspections, insurance, advertising, cross-marketing restrictions, approved products only, 24/7 operation, Hilton Honors participation, computer systems, approved consultants (5 types), etc.
May not be licensor/franchisor of competing brand during term
Must use approved management or approved Management Company. If MC becomes unsuitable, 90 days to replace.
Curable defaults: payment (10 days), construction timing (10 days), insurance (10 days), other FA breach (30 days)
16 categories of non-curable defaults allowing immediate termination without cure opportunity
Interim remedies on default: suspension from Reservation Service, directory removal, software disabling, fee accommodation revocation
Liquidated damages on termination: System or Hotel avg monthly royalty × up to 60 months. Forfeiture of all incentives.
Actual damages recoverable when franchisee operates competitor hotel at site within timeframes
All transfers prohibited unless expressly permitted. Two types: Permitted Transfers (no control change) and COO Transfers.
COO Transfer: 60 days notice, $150K fee, transferee must sign current FA, agree to PIP, meet all current requirements. No transfer to Competitor or Sanctioned Person.

Mortgages: simple hotel mortgage without consent if sole borrower and not cross-collateralized. All other mortgages require consent; may require lender comfort letter ($3,500 fee) with estoppel and general release.
During term: may not own or be licensor/franchisor of competing brand
Management Company restrictions: if MC becomes Competitor, 90 days to replace
NO post-term noncompete — explicitly not applicable

Guaranty required from principals/owners; may include spouse. Scope: all franchisee obligations under FA. Additional guaranty may be required on default notice or as condition of lender comfort letter.
Personal, unlimited guaranty of all FA obligations
Unauthorized opening: $5,000/day + costs
Pre-opening termination (franchisee builds competitor within 1 year): System Avg Monthly Royalty × 60
Post-opening termination (<2 years): Greater of Hotel or System Avg × 60
Post-opening termination (>2 years, before final 60 months): Hotel Avg × 60
Final 60 months: Hotel Avg × remaining months
Governing law: New York (without choice-of-law)
Venue: E.D. Va. (Alexandria) or Fairfax County, VA or New York, NY. Franchisor may sue where hotel is located.
WAIVER OF JURY TRIAL AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES — both parties waive right to jury trial and right to seek punitive, exemplary, consequential, or special damages

Franchisee must indemnify, hold harmless, and defend franchisor, Hilton Worldwide, and all affiliates/officers/directors/employees/agents against any claims arising from hotel operations or franchisee actions. Includes attorney fees and costs.

Upon signing FA, franchisee releases franchisor and all affiliates from all claims existing at time of execution (except FDD representation claims). This is a pre-signing release, not just a renewal/transfer release.
Must maintain minimum insurance per Manual (confidential). Must name franchisor as additional insured. If franchisee fails, franchisor may purchase and charge franchisee.
Consent/Business Judgment: Franchisor may withhold consent in its business judgment. Franchisee's sole remedy for unreasonable withholding is specific performance — NO money damages.
Economic Conditions Not a Defense: economic downturn, recession, or adverse market conditions are not a defense to franchisee's obligations
Confidentiality of Negotiated Terms: franchisee must keep all negotiated economic terms confidential
Ownership of Information: franchisor owns all data generated by or stored in computer systems
May lease/sublease commercial space in hotel subject to franchisor review and approval of business and brand/concept
